LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS - PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. DR. ARTUS IS NOT ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES PER CCP 1021.5 BECAUSE HER PURSUIT OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION DID NOT BENEFIT THE GENERAL PUBLIC OR A LARGE CLASS OR PERSONS. NONE OF THE STATUTES CITED BY DR. ARTUS AUTHORIZE AN AWARD OR INTERIM ATTORNEY'S FEES, NOR IS DR. ARTUS ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF INTERIM ATTORNEY'S FEES PER THE PARTIES' CONTRACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2018 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Iso Motion For Attys Fees
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Davis J. Millstein of the law offices of Millstein & Associates (415-348-0348) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Ann Rankin of the law offices of Ann Rankin (510-653-8886) appears as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party

The cause is submitted without argument by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court clarifies that Sanctions are against the association.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' Motion For Attorneys Fees and costs is GRANTED in part. Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association is required to pay $18,473.91 in fees and costs to Dr. Artus. If counsel for GTCA wishes to pay that amount instead of GTCA paying it, she may do so. It is undisputed that Dr. Artus prevailed on her motion for a preliminary injunction. That motion is fairly characterized as an action to enforce GTCA's governing documents since the enjoined Communications Rule effectively amended those governing documents. Thus, Dr. Artus is entitled to an award of reasonable fees and costs per Civil Code 5975(c). The fees sought by Dr. Artus are reasonable in both hours and hourly rate. No deduction from those fees is warranted because of the dismissal of the allegations regarding the fine schedule since it does not appear that the parties litigated the merits of any issue regarding the fine schedule. However, per Civil Code 5960, a deduction of $10,000 is warranted because Dr. Artus filed this lawsuit prior to seeking mediation or making a demand regarding the substance of the Communications Rule. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID J. MILLSTEIN OF MILLSTEIN & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-415-348-0348 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ANN DANKIN, TEL. NO. 1-510-653-8885 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS's Motion For Attorney's Fees and Costs - Plaintiff Kazuko Artus' motion for attorney's fees is denied. Dr. Artus is not entitled to an award of fees per CCP 1021.5 because her pursuit of the preliminary injunction did not benefit the general public or a large class or persons. None of the statutes cited by Dr. Artus authorize an award or interim attorney's fees, nor is Dr. Artus entitled to an award of interim attorney's fees per the parties' contract. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk
